Code fragment: var query = dsl.select()
Explanation: Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from the joined tables, which retrieves unnecessary data and impacts performance. Explicitly list only the required columns from FX_COURSE_DRAFT, e.g., `dsl.select(FX_COURSE_DRAFT.fields())`.

Code fragment:
var termCondition = DSL
                        .or(FX_COURSE_DRAFT.KEY.cast(String.class).containsIgnoreCase(term))
                        .or(FX_COURSE_DRAFT.TITLE.containsIgnoreCase(term))
                        .or(FX_COURSE_DRAFT.DESCRIPTION.containsIgnoreCase(term))
                        .or(FX_COURSE_DRAFT.AUTHOR.containsIgnoreCase(term));
Explanation: Using `containsIgnoreCase` (which translates to ILIKE with wildcards) for full-text search across multiple columns is inefficient and cannot use indexes. Consider using a proper full-text search solution like PostgreSQL's `tsvector`/`tsquery` or a dedicated search engine like Elasticsearch.

Code fragment:
var termCondition = DSL
    .or(FX_GROUP.KEY.cast(String.class).containsIgnoreCase(term))
    .or(FX_GROUP.NAME.containsIgnoreCase(term))
    .or(FX_GROUP.DESCRIPTION.containsIgnoreCase(term))
    .or(FX_GROUP.TYPE.containsIgnoreCase(term));
Explanation: Using containsIgnoreCase (which translates to LIKE '%term%') for full-text search is inefficient as it cannot use indexes and performs poorly on large datasets. Consider using a dedicated full-text search engine like PostgreSQL's tsvector/tsquery, Elasticsearch, or database-specific full-text search capabilities.

Code fragment: var result = dsl.selectFrom(FX_GROUP)
Explanation: Using selectFrom(FX_GROUP) fetches all columns from the FX_GROUP table, which may retrieve unnecessary data and impact performance. Consider explicitly selecting only the columns needed for the search results using dsl.select(...).from(FX_GROUP).

Code fragment:
var termCondition = DSL
    .or(FX_ROLE_ASSIGNMENT.KEY.cast(String.class).containsIgnoreCase(term))
    .or(FX_ROLE_ASSIGNMENT.STATUS.containsIgnoreCase(term))
    
    .or(FX_USER.KEY.cast(String.class).containsIgnoreCase(term))
    .or(FX_USER.USERNAME.containsIgnoreCase(term))
    .or(FX_USER.FIRST_NAME.containsIgnoreCase(term))
    .or(FX_USER.LAST_NAME.containsIgnoreCase(term))
    .or(FX_USER.EMAIL.containsIgnoreCase(term))
    
    .or(FX_GROUP.KEY.cast(String.class).containsIgnoreCase(term))
    .or(FX_GROUP.NAME.containsIgnoreCase(term))
    .or(FX_GROUP.DESCRIPTION.containsIgnoreCase(term));
Explanation: Using `containsIgnoreCase` (which translates to LIKE '%term%') across multiple columns for full-text search is inefficient and cannot use indexes. Consider using a dedicated full-text search engine like PostgreSQL's tsvector/tsquery, Elasticsearch, or a database-specific full-text index.
